items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Search.Documents` **Service directory:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/` **Triggered by:** commit 10b1ccf3e (PR #58411) by `@jorgerangel-msft` ## What Changed The push updates the TypeSpec/http-client-csharp emitter version and regenerates SDK code. The affected package `Azure.Search.Documents` 12.0.0 (released 2026-04-14) introduced `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` for agentic retrieval against knowledge bases — a major new feature — but the README has never been updated to document it. ## Gaps Found - The README has **no section** documenting `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` or the Knowledge Base Retrieval feature, despite it being the headline new addition in version 12.0.0. - A complete test-backed sample file already exists at `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` with seven `#region Snippet:` regions ready for inclusion, but none are referenced in the README. - The Examples table-of-contents in the README does not list Knowledge Base Retrieval. - The Key Concepts section has no description of knowledge bases, knowledge sources, or agentic retrieval.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/README.md` — **edit** 1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the existing Vector search sub-section, before Thread safety), add a new sub-section: ```markdown #### Knowledge base retrieval `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` provides agentic retrieval operations against Azure AI Search knowledge bases. A knowledge base is a managed collection of knowledge sources (search indexes, Azure Blobs, web content, OneLake) with an associated Azure OpenAI model that can synthesize responses to natural-language intents. Use `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` to query the knowledge base with semantic intents and receive synthesized answers with cited references. ``` 2. In the **Examples** table-of-contents block, add a new bullet after `* [Async APIs](#async-apis)`: ```markdown * [Knowledge base retrieval](#knowledge-base-retrieval) ``` 3. After the `### Async APIs` section (and before `### Authenticate in a National Cloud`), add a new section: ````markdown ### Knowledge base retrieval `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` enables agentic retrieval from an Azure AI Search knowledge base. First, add the required using directives: ```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces ``` Then create a client and issue a retrieval request with a natural-language intent: ```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve ``` ```` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet tool will populate the placeholder blocks above from the existing test file. No new test code needs to be written — `Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` already contains the required regions. | Snippet name | Test file location | Region start line | |---|---|---| | `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 10 | | `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 39 | Insert the placeholder blocks exactly as shown in Step 1 above. `Update-Snippets.ps1` will replace the content between the fences. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all six required top-level sections. After the change, confirm: - `## Key concepts` — now includes the new **Knowledge base retrieval** sub-section - `## Examples` — TOC now links to `#knowledge-base-retrieval` - New `### Knowledge base retrieval` section appears between `### Async APIs` and `### Authenticate in a National Cloud` Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order from the repository root: ```powershell dotnet build sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/src/ dotnet test sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/ --filter TestCategory!=Live eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 search eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 search dotnet format sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/ ``` After running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, verify with `git diff sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/README.md` that the two snippet placeholders were populated with the sample code.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Search] Docs: Add KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient documentation to README
